package.dependencies: none (zero third-party runtime dependencies, stdlib only)
package.not_a_runtime_engine: vsl-core does not intercept anything automatically. Every gate call, ledger write, and escalation is explicit code the developer (or a framework adapter) writes and calls.

primitives.name: PreNode
primitives.purpose: Gates a single guarded transition before it happens.

derived_properties.name: assurance_level
derived_properties.note: read-only, derived from assurance_basis.derived_level -- cannot be set directly

primitives.name: Invariant
primitives.purpose: An always-on hard constraint, checked independently of any PreNode. Violation raises InvariantViolation, not a bare exception.

fields.name: on_violation
fields.note: names which TerminalState a violation transitions into

primitives.name: InvariantViolation
primitives.purpose: Raised specifically when an Invariant's rule returns False -- not a bare AutomationDeniedException, so callers can distinguish 'no Fallback path, straight to Terminal' from an ordinary PreNode Fallback exhaustion.

fields.name: terminal_state_name
fields.note: the TerminalState's name if Invariant.on_violation was set, else None. A plain string, not the real TerminalState object -- keeps exceptions.py dependency-free, same reasoning as LedgerEntry.identity_key being a plain string. Structurally distinguishes 'a violation that also halted the system' from 'a violation with no terminal consequence' -- previously only recoverable by parsing the free-text reason string.

primitives.name: TerminalState
primitives.purpose: A state from which no further automated transition is permitted without human authorisation.

primitives.name: AllowedState
primitives.purpose: A classified outcome that met all required prerequisites.

primitives.name: InadmissibleState
primitives.purpose: A named, classified outcome that must not occur. Descriptive/documentation object -- does not carry an assurance_basis (deliberate asymmetry with AllowedState). The actual runtime block is whatever PreNode/Invariant prevents reaching it.

fields.name: signature
fields.note: free-text description of the detection pattern, e.g. 'provider.status == SUSPENDED'

primitives.name: Fallback
primitives.purpose: Retry/escalation policy attached to a PreNode.

primitives.name: Cluster
primitives.purpose: Aggregates constituent systems by IdentityKey (not by Instance) for multi-agent risk. Raises ValueError at construction if a declared constituent isn't covered by its own cluster_key.

methods.name: add_constituent(identity_key)
methods.note: returns a new Cluster with the constituent added and cluster_key recomputed

primitives.name: IdentityKey
primitives.purpose: A stable identifier scoping a single system/agent/instance.

primitives.name: ClusterKey
primitives.purpose: Composite key for a group of constituent systems. NOT a subclass of IdentityKey -- a cluster-level key must never be silently accepted where a single-system key is expected.

classmethods.name: from_constituents(constituent_keys)
classmethods.note: deterministic hash of sorted constituent values -- same set of constituents always produces the same key regardless of order

primitives.name: Evidence
primitives.purpose: Required evidence for recovery/re-enablement. Both fields validated non-empty at construction.

primitives.name: Instance
primitives.purpose: A single execution of a process, scoped by IdentityKey.

methods.name: _re_enable(*, evidence)
methods.warning: bare mechanism, no authority check -- directly callable by anyone with an Evidence object despite the leading underscore. The underscore itself is the fix for a real gap: this was a public re_enable() until an external review flagged it as an easy accidental-bypass risk. Prefer governance.request_re_enablement() for the audited path.

primitives.name: GovernanceAuthority
primitives.purpose: A named role/body authorised to approve exceptions and transitions.

primitives.name: HumanAuthorisedTransition
primitives.purpose: An auditable, explicit human-approval record. The only sanctioned way out of a TerminalState.

fields.name: authorised_by
fields.note: must be a real, non-empty human approver name

primitives.name: request_re_enablement
primitives.kind: module-level function (NOT a method on GovernanceAuthority)
primitives.purpose: The sanctioned path out of a TerminalState. Pairs a new Instance with the HumanAuthorisedTransition record that authorises it.
primitives.signature: request_re_enablement(instance: Instance, authority: GovernanceAuthority, *, authorised_by: str, evidence: Evidence) -> tuple[Instance, HumanAuthorisedTransition]

primitives.name: Proofing
primitives.purpose: Result of an automated verification that a claimed attribute matches an authoritative record. Plain result type -- caller supplies the domain-specific comparison.

primitives.name: Binding
primitives.purpose: Links a verified Proofing to an approved escalation path. Cannot be constructed from an unverified Proofing (raises ValueError).

primitives.name: Specification
primitives.purpose: Policy-as-Code: a versioned, machine-readable bundle of PreNodes/Invariants/TerminalStates. Requires Proofing + GOV approval before activation.

methods.name: activate()
methods.note: raises ValueError if already active or if no Binding attached

primitives.name: VerbaLedger
primitives.purpose: Hash-chained, append-only audit log.

methods.name: write(entry_type, *, identity_key, cluster_key=None, instance_id=None, decision_id=None, caused_by=None, payload=None)
write_monitor(*, identity_key, drift_detected, cluster_key=None, instance_id=None, decision_id=None, caused_by=None, extra_payload=None)
write_verification(*, identity_key, result: VerificationResult, cluster_key=None, instance_id=None, decision_id=None, caused_by=None, extra_payload=None)
verify_integrity()
methods.note: never raises -- recomputes every entry's hash and chain pointer

methods.name: audit(*, max_monitor_gap_seconds=None)
methods.note: 5 named checks; checks 2-5 match causally via caused_by when set, else fall back to identity/instance/timestamp heuristic

methods.name: issue_certificate(*, max_monitor_gap_seconds=None)
methods.note: returns None if any audit check fails -- never fabricates

methods.name: current_checkpoint()
methods.note: chain's current tip (sequence, entry_hash, checked_at) for external witnessing -- does no anchoring/signing itself

important_gotchas: decision_id/caused_by are optional -- populate caused_by whenever one identity/instance can have more than one decision in flight at once, or audit() can mismatch an unrelated entry as resolving the wrong one.

Hash-chaining is tamper-evidence, not cryptographic signing -- it proves an entry wasn't altered after being written, not who wrote it or that the chain wasn't truncated/replaced wholesale. current_checkpoint() is the seam for adding external protection, not the protection itself.

primitives.name: AssuranceBasis
primitives.purpose: The F1/F2 facts an AssuranceLevel is derived from. Required on PreNode/Invariant/AllowedState -- assurance_level can never be set directly.

fields.name: f1_pre_commitment
fields.note: does the check run before the action commits?

fields.name: f2_modification
fields.note: how completely does the check modify the actual outcome vs. just touching a prompt/sampling parameter?

derived_properties.name: derived_level
derived_properties.table: F1=False -> LOW (any F2). F1=True + FULL -> HIGH. F1=True + PARTIAL/INDIRECT -> MEDIUM. F1=True + NONE -> LOW.

primitives.name: GammaEstimate
primitives.purpose: What a PreNode's monitor callable returns.

methods.name: sufficient(threshold=1.1)
methods.note: this is what a compiled PreNode gate actually checks

primitives.name: VSLAdapter
primitives.purpose: The contract a framework-specific adapter must implement to compile PreNode/Invariant objects into real, callable async gates.
primitives.compiled_gate_shape: async def gate(candidate_input) -> None -- raises AutomationDeniedException/InvariantViolation on failure, returns normally on pass

primitives.name: PlainPythonReferenceAdapter
primitives.purpose: The default adapter when no framework-specific one exists or is needed -- direct Python calls, zero framework underneath.

primitives.name: run_conformance_suite
primitives.purpose: Mechanically proves an adapter blocks-before-side-effect, passes-through-when-sufficient, raises the specific InvariantViolation type, and is safely reusable. Empty list == conformant.

decision_heuristic.purpose: How to find governance decision points in an arbitrary agent, before knowing which primitive to reach for.

signals.signal: A side effect on an external system (payment, email send, database write, infra change -- anything not purely in-memory)
signals.primitive: PreNode (sometimes Invariant if it must never be bypassed regardless of context)
signals.rationale: Gate the action before it fires, checked against a monitor's GammaEstimate.

signals.signal: An irreversibility boundary (delete, refund, transfer -- anything that can't be cleanly undone)
signals.rationale: These tend to be the 'cannot be bypassed' cases -- always-on, not context-dependent gating.

signals.signal: A point where untrusted input could redirect what the agent does next (a tool's return value, retrieved content, another agent's message)
signals.primitive: PreNode gating the next action (not the input itself)
signals.rationale: vsl-core does not sanitize input -- it gates what happens after, so the PreNode's monitor should check the proposed next action against the declared scope.

signals.signal: A scope/permission escalation (the agent about to act outside what was declared for this task)
signals.primitive: Invariant, often paired with Identity (IdentityKey/Instance) and Cluster if multiple agents are involved
signals.rationale: Escalation is usually a hard 'never' rule, and identity scoping is what makes 'declared for this task' checkable at all.

decision_heuristic.when_the_use_case_is_vague: If the risky/irreversible actions in the stated use case aren't obvious from a one-line prompt, ask 1-2 clarifying questions about what's genuinely external-facing or irreversible in that domain before constructing anything. Do not guess and silently assume.

canonical_patterns.name: Gate a high-impact action before it runs
canonical_patterns.code:
from vsl_core.constructs import PreNode, Fallback
from vsl_core.metrics import AssuranceBasis, F2Modification, GammaEstimate

ALLOWED_TOOLS = {"lookup_order", "issue_refund_under_50"}

async def within_declared_scope(candidate_action) -> GammaEstimate:
    ok = candidate_action.tool_name in ALLOWED_TOOLS
    return GammaEstimate(gamma_hat=2.0 if ok else 0.1)

tool_call_gate = PreNode(
    name="tool_call_gate",
    description="A tool call only proceeds if it's within this agent's declared scope",
    monitor=within_declared_scope,
    assurance_basis=AssuranceBasis(f1_pre_commitment=True, f2_modification=F2Modification.FULL),
    fallback=Fallback(max_retries=1, on_max_retries="AUTOMATION_DENIED"),
)

canonical_patterns.name: A hard rule that can't be bypassed, with an escalation path
canonical_patterns.code:
from vsl_core.constructs import Invariant, TerminalState
from vsl_core.metrics import AssuranceBasis, F2Modification

governance_halt = TerminalState(
    name="governance_halt",
    description="Halts pending human review after a hard rule is violated",
    entry_conditions=("invariant_violated",),
)

async def payment_is_verified(candidate) -> bool:
    return candidate.customer.payment_verified is True

payment_required = Invariant(
    name="payment_required",
    description="Never confirm a booking without a verified payment method",
    rule=payment_is_verified,
    assurance_basis=AssuranceBasis(f1_pre_commitment=True, f2_modification=F2Modification.FULL),
    on_violation=governance_halt,
)

canonical_patterns.name: Identity scoping and a human-authorised way back into operation
canonical_patterns.code:
from vsl_core.identity import Evidence
from vsl_core.governance import GovernanceAuthority, request_re_enablement

risk_committee = GovernanceAuthority(name="Risk Committee", escalation_contact="risk@example.com")

evidence = Evidence(
    root_cause_analysis="Booking gate blocked all candidates; provider cache was stale.",
    specification_update_proposal="Refresh the provider cache every 5 minutes instead of hourly.",
)

new_instance, transition = request_re_enablement(
    suspended_instance,
    risk_committee,
    authorised_by="j.smith",
    evidence=evidence,
)
# Both the new Instance and the HumanAuthorisedTransition record exist together --
# a caller can't get one without the other.

canonical_patterns.name: A tamper-evident, auditable record of what happened
canonical_patterns.code:
from vsl_core.ledger import VerbaLedger, VerificationResult

ledger = VerbaLedger()
ledger.write_monitor(identity_key=str(agent_identity), drift_detected=False)
ledger.write_verification(identity_key=str(agent_identity), result=VerificationResult.SUFFICIENT)

report = ledger.audit()
certificate = ledger.issue_certificate()  # None if any of the 5 audit checks fail -- never fabricated

canonical_patterns.name: Multi-agent aggregation that can't silently omit a member
canonical_patterns.code:
from vsl_core.cluster import Cluster
from vsl_core.identity import IdentityKey, ClusterKey

agents = (IdentityKey.generate("booking"), IdentityKey.generate("payments"))
cluster_key = ClusterKey.from_constituents(agents)

checkout_cluster = Cluster(name="checkout_flow", cluster_key=cluster_key, constituents=agents)
# Constructing a Cluster whose constituents aren't covered by its own cluster_key
# raises ValueError immediately.

steps: 1. Identify decision points using the decision_heuristic (ask clarifying questions if the use case is vague).
2. Construct PreNode/Invariant/TerminalState/Cluster objects -- pure data plus callables, nothing is live yet.
3. Compile them into callable gates via an adapter conforming to VSLAdapter (PlainPythonReferenceAdapter unless a specific framework adapter is named).
4. Call the compiled gate immediately before the real action. It raises on failure, returns normally on pass.
5. Write what happened to a VerbaLedger (write_monitor/write_verification/write) -- this is never automatic.
6. Periodically call audit()/issue_certificate() for a compliance-shaped report.
7. If a TerminalState/Invariant.on_violation fires, the only sanctioned way back is governance.request_re_enablement().

automatic: The compiled gate raising AutomationDeniedException/InvariantViolation when its check fails.
The hash chain updating and verify_integrity() detecting tampering.
audit()'s 5 checks running against whatever entries exist.
issue_certificate() refusing to fabricate a certificate on a failed audit.
manual_the_developer_must_write: Catching the raised exception -- vsl-core does not catch it for you.
Writing the TERMINAL ledger entry when a TerminalState is entered.
Actually halting further execution -- there is no running 'current state' object that gets flipped; the calling code decides what happens next.
Calling request_re_enablement() and writing the resulting RE_ENABLEMENT/HUMAN_AUTHORISED_TRANSITION entries.
Populating decision_id/caused_by when more than one decision can be in flight per identity/instance.

generic_scaffold.purpose: The repeatable project structure for wiring vsl-core into a new, arbitrary use case. Domain-agnostic -- substitute the real nouns/actions of whatever use case is stated.

files.contains: Plain dataclasses modeling the domain's entities and their states (e.g. Customer, Order, ProviderStatus).
The actual functions that perform side-effecting actions -- the 'controlled tools' this domain needs (e.g. issue_refund, send_email, book_appointment).

Constructs the PreNode/Invariant/TerminalState/InadmissibleState/Cluster objects for this domain, one per decision point identified via decision_heuristic. This is where AssuranceBasis is justified for each construct -- not defaulted.
Orchestrates the flow: decide what to do -> compile gates via an adapter -> call gates before each tools.py action -> write ledger entries -> catch exceptions and handle TerminalState/re-enablement.
Prove the governance shape: each PreNode/Invariant actually blocks the case it should and passes the case it shouldn't, using PlainPythonReferenceAdapter directly.

procedure_for_an_ai_following_this: If the risky/irreversible actions in the stated use case aren't obvious, ask before guessing.
List the domain's key actions and classify each via decision_heuristic.signals.
Write governance.py, justifying each AssuranceBasis with real F1/F2 facts, not a default HIGH.
Write agent.py wiring PlainPythonReferenceAdapter (or a named framework adapter) + VerbaLedger + explicit exception handling per lifecycle.automatic_vs_manual.
Write tests proving each gate blocks/passes correctly.
State plainly which of this document's canonical_patterns each part of the generated code corresponds to, so the developer can verify it against this reference.

common_mistakes: Setting assurance_level directly instead of deriving it from AssuranceBasis -- raises TypeError, it's a read-only property.
Assuming a TerminalState or ledger write happens automatically when a gate raises -- it doesn't; the calling code must catch the exception and write the entry itself.
Reusing one Instance across multiple concurrent decisions without decision_id/caused_by -- can make audit() mismatch an unrelated entry as resolving the wrong one.
Treating the Drift Class catalog (vsl_core.catalog) as an active scanner -- it's a reference taxonomy a monitor/rule function can consult, not executable detection logic on its own.
Calling Instance._re_enable() directly instead of governance.request_re_enablement() -- bypasses the authority/evidence audit trail.
Distinguishing a plain InvariantViolation from one that also entered a TerminalState by parsing the reason string instead of checking the exception's terminal_state_name field directly.
Describing VerbaLedger's hash chain as a cryptographic signature or proof of non-repudiation -- it's tamper-evidence (detects change after the fact), not identity-bound signing.
Claiming HIGH assurance for a mechanism that only touches a prompt or sampling parameter behind a hosted API -- that caps at MEDIUM (F1=True, F2=PARTIAL).
